42.gl: familiar; a friend, also gentle; hence, a bullock (as being tame; applied, although masculine, to a cow); and so, a chieftain (as notable, like neat cattle)
42.kj: captain, duke, (chief) friend, governor, guide, ox.

42.gl: Teman, the name of two Edomites, and of the region and descendant of one of them
42.dv: or תֵּמָן; the same as H8486 (תֵּימָן);

42.gl: Kenaz, the name of an Edomite and of two Israelites
42.dv: probably from an unused root meaning to hunt; hunter;

43.gl: a seat; figuratively, a site; abstractly, a session; by extension an abode (the place or the time); by implication, population
43.kj: assembly, dwell in, dwelling(-place), wherein (that) dwelt (in), inhabited place, seat, sitting, situation, sojourning.

43.gl: familiar; a friend, also gentle; hence, a bullock (as being tame; applied, although masculine, to a cow); and so, a chieftain (as notable, like neat cattle)
43.kj: captain, duke, (chief) friend, governor, guide, ox.

43.gl: the earth (at large, or partitively a land)
43.kj: [idiom] common, country, earth, field, ground, land, [idiom] natins, way, [phrase] wilderness, world.
43.dv: from an unused root probably meaning to be firm;

43.gl: Esav, a son of Isaac, including his posterity
43.dv: apparently a form of the passive participle of H6213 (עָשָׂה) in the original sense of handling; rough (i.e. sensibly felt);

43.gl: father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application
43.kj: chief, (fore-) father(-less), [idiom] patrimony, principal. Compare names in 'Abi-'.

43.gl: Edom, the elder twin-brother of Jacob; hence the region (Idumaea) occupied by him
43.dv: or (fully) אֱדוֹם ; from H122 (אָדֹם); red (see Genesis 25:25);
